hero.title: A living space for art, research, and collective imagination.
hero.subtitle: A living space for art, research, and collective imagination.

about.title: About
about.body: Gihanga Institute of Contemporary Art is a non-profit centre for the arts that is home to a library, exhibitions, platforms for performance, a screening room, studio, and art residency. The 777 sqm institute is dedicated to supporting Rwandan and regional artists through thoughtful exhibitions, shared spaces for making, and commitment to intellectual and artistic exchange.

welcome.title: Closing 7-14 April in honour of the victims of the Genocide against the Tusti.
welcome.subtitle: Closing 7-14 April in honour of the victims of the Genocide against the Tusti.

contributors.title: Contributors
contributors.subtitle: We would like to thank the following individuals and organizations who have supported us along the way, which has helped us build and provide a solid foundation for artistic experiment

benefactor.heading: FOUNDING BENEFACTOR
benefactor.body: We are grateful for the generous support extended to us by the Mellon Foundation, our Founding Benefactor.
